fields.equipmentProficiencies:
Darkvision: 60ft
Swim Speed: 30ft 
Weapons: Simple
Tools: Calligrapher's Supplies
fields.treasureNotes:
Calligrapher's Supplies
Fine Clothes
Book about Thalassic Mythology
Arcane Focus (crystal)
Dungeoneer's Pack
fields.equipmentInventory: Spear, 2 Daggers, Backpack, Caltrops, Crowbar, 2 flasks of Oil, 10 days of Rations, Rope, Tinderbox, 10 Torches, and Waterskin.

feats.name: Goose: Golden Egg
feats.details: Whenever you make a critical hit, you can take or give a heroic inspiration. Giving can only be done to allies within 30 ft of you.

feats.name: Goose: Corrupted Egg
feats.short: At 1/2 health, DC15 dex save or 2d8 force and 10ft push.
feats.details: When you are equal to or below 1/2th of health, you explode in frustration. Unleashing a burst of energy with a 15 ft radius around you (once per long rest). Those within your vicinity (allies and enemies alike) will need to make a dexterity saving throw (DC 15). Upon failure, they’ll be pushed 10 ft away and take 2D8 force damage. Upon a successful save, they’ll only take half the damage.

feats.name: Amphibious
feats.short: Ability to breathe and move above and below water.
feats.details:
Aeternians have grown gills, webbing between their phalanges, and translucent pearl-like scales which become  
visible underwater; allowing them to breathe and swim underwater no different than on land.

feats.name: Cold Climate Adept
feats.details:
Aeternians have adapted to the cold of the deep, allowing them to become unbothered by cold climate  
temperatures, though they may require more hydration in warmer climates.

feats.name: Bioluminescence
feats.short: 5/15ft of emitting dim light in natural darkness.
feats.details:
Before the Aeternians had discovered how to light their underwater Queendom, they themselves emitted light  in darkness through bioluminescence.  

When in natural darkness, the scales of the Aeternians softly exude a dim light. On land their scales are less  on the surface of their skin; therefore they only emit dim light for 5 ft, while underwater their scales surface,  due to which the Aeternians’ bioluminescence is strengthened to emitting dim light for 15 ft. The  bioluminescence does not shine within magical darkness.  

The bioluminescence effect comes from releasing the stored light within the scales of the Aeternian.  
Generally, 6–8 hours of exposure to a light source is required to store enough light to generate the effect.  As such, in covert situations, Aeternians who do not wish to shed light will ensure to either not be exposed  to a light source, or to fully cover themselves. However, the lack of natural light is regarded as an indication  of shady business; an Aeternian who does not emit bioluminescence is commonly viewed with suspicion  
by others of their kin.

feats.name: Aquatic Affiliation
feats.details: Due to centuries of living below the water surface, the Aeternians have become adept in communing and  handling aquatic beasts.

feats.name: Siren's Call
feats.short: Can cast Siren's Call equal to proficiency bonus.
feats.details:
The geographical change of the Aeternia Queendom came with new forms of danger. However, due to their  brilliant minds and abundant forms of art, they quickly became connected to the Sirens, a species that are  natural denizens of the sea.  

Through them they learned the Siren's Call, a method to warn others while forcing their assailants to move  away from them. They can cast this spell of warding a number of times equal to their proficiency bonus  per long rest.  

Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is their spellcasting ability for this trait (choose when you select this race). -> Charisma

feats.name: Versatile: Cleric Initiate
feats.short: Guidance & Spare the Dying, Healing Word.
feats.details:
Aeternians are as versatile as their surface-born counterparts: humans. Depending on their origin they gain  a matching feat (gain an Origin feat of choice). -> Cleric Initiate

Two Cantrips. You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Cleric, Druid, or Wizard spell list. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells (choose when you select this feat). -> Charisma; Guidance & Spare the Dying

Level 1 Spell. Choose a level 1 spell from the same list you selected for this feat's cantrips. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have. -> Healing Word

Spell Change. Whenever you gain a new level, you can replace one of the spells you chose for this feat with a different spell of the same level from the chosen spell list.

Repeatable. You can take this feat more than once, but you must choose a different spell list each time.

feats.name: Resourceful
feats.short: Gain Heroic Inspiration at Long Rest.
feats.details: Similar to their surface-born counterparts (humans), Aeternians gain Heroic Inspiration whenever they finish  a Long Rest.

feats.name: Innate Sorcery
feats.short: Spell save DC +1 and advantage on attack rolls.
feats.details:
An event in your past left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with simmering magic. As a Bonus Action, you can unleash that magic for 1 minute, during which you gain the following benefits:

The spell save DC of your Sorcerer spells increases by 1.
You have Advantage on the attack rolls of Sorcerer spells you cast.
You can use this feature twice, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a Long Rest.

feats.name: Font of Magic
feats.details:
You can tap into the wellspring of magic within yourself. This wellspring is represented by Sorcery Points, which allow you to create a variety of magical effects.

You have 2 Sorcery Points, and you gain more as you reach higher levels, as shown in the Sorcery Points column of the Sorcerer Features table. You can’t have more Sorcery Points than the number shown in the table for your level. You regain all expended Sorcery Points when you finish a Long Rest.

You can use your Sorcery Points to fuel the options below, along with other features, such as Metamagic, that use those points.

Converting Spell Slots to Sorcery Points. You can expend a spell slot to gain a number of Sorcery Points equal to the slot’s level (no action required).

Creating Spell Slots. As a Bonus Action, you can transform unexpended Sorcery Points into one spell slot. The Creating Spell Slots table shows the cost of creating a spell slot of a given level, and it lists the minimum Sorcerer level you must be to create a slot. You can create a spell slot no higher than level 5.

Any spell slot you create with this feature vanishes when you finish a Long Rest.

Creating Spell Slots
Spell Slot Level	Sorcery Point Cost	Min. Sorcerer Level
1	2	2
2	3	3
3	5	5
4	6	7
5	7	9

spells.name: Guidance (Cleric Initiate)
spells.effect: 1d4 to any ability check for 1 min
spells.details:
Divination Cantrip (Artificer, Cleric, Druid)

Casting Time: Action
Range: Touch
Components: V, S
Duration: Concentration, up to 1 minute

You touch a willing creature and choose a skill. Until the spell ends, the creature adds 1d4 to any ability check using the chosen skill.

spells.name: Spare the Dying (Cleric Initiate)
spells.effect: A creature with 0 hit points becomes stable
spells.details:
Necromancy Cantrip (Artificer, Cleric, Druid)

Casting Time: Action
Range: 15 feet
Components: V, S
Duration: Instantaneous

Choose a creature within range that has 0 Hit Points and isn’t dead. The creature becomes Stable.

Cantrip Upgrade. The range doubles when you reach levels 5 (30 feet), 11 (60 feet), and 17 (120 feet).

spells.name: Sorcerous Burst
spells.effect: 1d8 type of choice. Add d8 if you roll an 8.
spells.details:
Evocation Cantrip (Sorcerer)

Casting Time: Action
Range: 120 feet
Components: V, S
Duration: Instantaneous

You cast sorcerous energy at one creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 damage of a type you choose: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Psychic, or Thunder.

If you roll an 8 on a d8 for this spell, you can roll another d8, and add it to the damage. When you cast this spell, the maximum number of these d8s you can add to the spell’s damage equals your spellcasting ability modifier.

Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d8 when you reach levels 5 (2d8), 11 (3d8), and 17 (4d8).

spells.name: Mage Hand
spells.details:
Conjuration Cantrip (Artificer, Bard, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard)

Casting Time: Action
Range: 30 feet
Components: V, S
Duration: 1 minute

A spectral, floating hand appears at a point you choose within range. The hand lasts for the duration. The hand vanishes if it is ever more than 30 feet away from you or if you cast this spell again.

When you cast the spell, you can use the hand to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial.

As a Magic action on your later turns, you can control the hand thus again. As part of that action, you can move the hand up to 30 feet.

The hand can’t attack, activate magic items, or carry more than 10 pounds.

spells.name: Mending
spells.effect: Repair a break or tear no larger than 1 foot
spells.details:
Transmutation Cantrip (Bard, Cleric, Druid, Sorcerer, Wizard)

Casting Time: 1 minute
Range: Touch
Components: V, S, M (two lodestones)
Duration: Instantaneous

This spell repairs a single break or tear in an object you touch, such as a broken chain link, two halves of a broken key, a torn cloak, or a leaking wineskin. As long as the break or tear is no larger than 1 foot in any dimension, you mend it, leaving no trace of the former damage.

This spell can physically repair a magic item, but it can’t restore magic to such an object.

spells.name: Prestidigitation
spells.details:
Transmutation Cantrip (Artificer, Bard, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard)

Casting Time: Action
Range: 10 feet
Components: V, S
Duration: Up to 1 hour

You create a magical effect within range. Choose the effect from the options below. If you cast this spell multiple times, you can have up to three of its non-instantaneous effects active at a time.

Sensory Effect. You create an instantaneous, harmless sensory effect, such as a shower of sparks, a puff of wind, faint musical notes, or an odd odor.

Fire Play. You instantaneously light or snuff out a candle, a torch, or a small campfire.

Clean or Soil. You instantaneously clean or soil an object no larger than 1 cubic foot.

Minor Sensation. You chill, warm, or flavor up to 1 cubic foot of nonliving material for 1 hour.

Magic Mark. You make a color, a small mark, or a symbol appear on an object or a surface for 1 hour.

Minor Creation. You create a nonmagical trinket or an illusory image that can fit in your hand. It lasts until the end of your next turn. A trinket can deal no damage and has no monetary worth.

spells.name: Siren's Call (Aeternian)
spells.effect: Con save; 15/20 ft cone of 2d8 thunder and pushed 15/20 ft
spells.details:
Spell Level: 1st
Casting Time: 1 Action
Range/Area: Self — 15 ft. cone
Components: V, S

Duration: Instantaneous
School: Evocation
Attack/Save: Constitution Save
Damage/Effect: Thunder

Description
Through the echoes, your call for help shall be heard. A defensive spell created by the magic researchers of the Aeternia kingdom. As you cry for help, you forcefully push away those who mean you harm. However, be mindful, as sound transfers differently under water.

On Land
Each creature within a 15 ft. cone makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they take 2d8 Thunder damage and are pushed away from you by 5 ft. On a successful save, they take half the damage and are not pushed.

Your call travels over 100 ft. on land.

Under Water
Each creature within a 20 ft. cone makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they take 2d8 Thunder damage and are pushed away from you by 15 ft. On a successful save, they take half the damage and are not pushed.

Your call travels over 300 ft. through water.

Higher-Level Spell Slot
The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

spells.name: Healing Word (Cleric Initiate)
spells.details:
Level 1 Abjuration (Bard, Cleric, Druid)

Casting Time: Bonus Action
Range: 60 feet
Components: V
Duration: Instantaneous

A creature of your choice that you can see within range regains Hit Points equal to 2d4 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing increases by 2d4 for each spell slot level above 1.

spells.name: Bubble Spray
spells.effect: Wis save; cone of 1d6 psychic damage and blinded
spells.details:
Spell Level: 1st
Casting Time: 1 Action
Range/Area: Self — 15 ft. cone
Components: V, S, M*
*A small ring

Duration: Instantaneous
School: Evocation
Attack/Save: Wisdom Save
Damage/Effect: Psychic

Description
You spray a dazzling collection of mystifying bubbles from your hands. Each creature in a 15 ft. cone, originating from you, must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become blinded until the end of your next turn. In addition, upon a failed save, the creatures take 1d6 Psychic damage, or half as much on a successful save.

Natural fires within the cone will be extinguished.

Higher-Level Spell Slot
The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

spells.name: Whirlpool
spells.effect: Dex save; 3d8/3d10 cold damage and target falls prone/is pushed downward
spells.details:
Spell Level: 1st
Casting Time: 1 Action
Range/Area: 90 ft. — 5 ft. square
Components: S, M*
*A small vial of water

Duration: Instantaneous
School: Conjuration
Attack/Save: Dexterity Save
Damage/Effect: Cold & Control

Description
As you spin around your vial of water, a whirlpool comes to be. Uncorking the vial will release said whirlpool upon a target within range (90 ft.). The target must make a Dexterity saving throw or experience the full effect of the spell. The effect of the spell differs depending on whether the target is on the surface or below it:

On Land
The target takes 3d8 cold damage and falls prone. On a successful save, the target takes half the damage and does not fall prone.

Under Water
The target takes 3d10 cold damage and is pushed downward by 30 ft. On a successful save, the target takes half the damage and is not pushed downward.

Higher-Level Spell Slot
For each spell slot above level 1, an extra whirlpool is created.
